dialogueChunks.intro: The alliance between our settlement and the one at {location} is over. Not because of war. Because of betrayal.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our trade representative was caught secretly negotiating with the {enemy_type} to undercut the partner settlement's prices. It wasn't an accident or a misunderstanding: it was a calculated violation of every covenant we swore. The partner settlement has severed all contact. {quest_focus} took years to build and was destroyed in a single meeting.
dialogueChunks.outro: The plans they stole from the partner settlement are still in the hands of the {enemy_type}. If someone could {quest_action}, it would prove we're willing to make it right. Without that gesture, {quest_threat} is all we have to look forward to.

playerResponses.text: Broken trust is hard to mend, but returning what was stolen is a start.
Why should I clean up your representative's mess?
You broke the alliance. Live with the consequences.

dialogueChunks.intro: The guard captain swore an oath to protect this settlement. She's been selling patrol schedules to the {enemy_type} for six months.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three ambushes in two months, all targeting patrols at their most vulnerable positions. It couldn't be coincidence. {quest_focus} was only discovered because a wounded scout overheard a {enemy_type} raider laughing about knowing exactly when the guard changes happen.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need someone from outside the garrison to {quest_action}. If the guards learn about this from their own ranks, it'll tear the unit apart. Hard evidence presented to the council is the only way to remove her without destroying {quest_stakes}.

playerResponses.text: An oath-breaker in command is worse than any enemy. I'll find the proof.
What if the proof implicates others too?

dialogueChunks.intro: The founding covenant guaranteed every family an equal share of the settlement's land. Someone has replaced the original document with a forgery that gives the founding family majority ownership.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Twelve families received eviction notices this morning. The forged charter is the legal basis. Nobody knows when the original was swapped, but {quest_focus} is convincing enough that the regional authority accepted it. The real document was likely hidden or destroyed.
dialogueChunks.outro: If someone can {quest_action} from {quest_origin}, or find proof it was altered, the evictions stop. {quest_threat} starts at the end of the week. Those families have nowhere to go.

playerResponses.text: Forging a community's founding document is the worst kind of theft. I'll help.
If the original is destroyed, what then?
Legal disputes aren't really my expertise.
